Prologue

  • Narration
    One moment you were sparring. The next, the sky was above you and the stone was beneath you and your buddy's hands were locked around your wrists.
  • Narration
    Clean takedown. Fast. You hadn't even seen the shift in their weight before you were on the ground. Your buddy leaned over you, breathing hard from the match, and you waited for them to let go the way they always did.
  • Narration
    They didn't let go.
  • Narration
    Their grip stayed firm. Their weight settled. And when you looked up, their expression had changed -- the easy grin from the match was gone, replaced by something quieter and sharper. Focused. Like they'd stopped sparring and started doing something else entirely.
  • Narration
    The courtyard was empty. The sky was deepening into night. Their hands were warm around your wrists, and you could feel their pulse through their fingers -- or maybe it was yours.
  • Narration
    You knew three ways to break this pin. You thought about none of them.

  • Narration
    The seventh edge crests and shatters without withdrawal. Konoko's thumb presses down—firm, final, unrelenting—and holds.
  • Konoko
    "Shatter." One word. Absolute. His fingers curl deep and stay, thumb grinding down with the full weight of every denial that came before.
  • Narration
    Spierce's spine arches hard against Konoko's chest, her broken stutter dissolving into a raw, wordless cry. The climax tears through her in waves—violent, layered, built from six edges of compounded denial crashing down at once. Her hands claw at nothing. Her thighs shake uncontrollably.
  • Konoko
    "Kohkohkoh♪ There she goes." His voice is barely audible against her hair, thick with dark satisfaction. His fingers don't stop—riding every spasm, pressing into every aftershock with merciless precision. "Every single one, darlin'. Give me every single one."
  • Narration
    The release lasts longer than any before it—drawn out by Konoko's refusal to withdraw, his thumb still circling through the wreckage of her composure. Spierce's body jolts and shudders against him in waves that seem to have no end, each one wrung from her by fingers that know exactly where to stay and exactly how long to hold.
  • Konoko
    "Good girl." His touch gentles at last—fingers easing, thumb slowing to a featherlight graze. His arm around her ribs loosens from vise to cradle, pulling her slack body flush against his chest. "Ya broke so pretty for me. Kohkohkoh♪ Mindless and dumb, just like ya wanted."
  • Konoko
    His hand withdraws slowly—tracing her inner thigh on the way out, possessive even in retreat. He brings his fingers to her jaw, tilting her head back against his shoulder so he can see her face. His thumb brushes her parted lips. "Still in there, darlin'?" His smile is quiet, satisfied, and utterly gentle. "Take yer time. I ain't goin' nowhere."
  • Narration
    Spierce's breathing comes in shallow, shuddering gasps. Her eyes are glassy, unfocused, fixed on the stars above without seeing them. Konoko's heartbeat is steady against her spine—an anchor in the wreckage of sensation. The courtyard is silent except for the sound of her slowly returning to herself. What will Spierce do?

Turn 21

  • Spierce
    I'm absolutely dazed as I return to myself, and the first thing that comes out is a drunk giggle.

    "Ehehe...~ You're so, sooo good, Konoko..." I press a series of kisses to his throat, pleased by how severe and merciless he can be.
  • Narration
    Spierce's lips find the warm skin of Konoko's throat, each kiss landing soft and unhurried. A low vibration passes through his chest beneath her back—not quite a laugh, not quite a sound, just the resonance of someone absorbing praise he never learned how to expect.
  • Konoko
    "Kohkohkoh♪ 'Good,' she says." His hand cradles the back of her skull, fingers threading through sweat-damp hair. His voice is low, stripped of its usual sharpness—just warmth and the faint roughness of someone whose composure cracked an inch without him noticing. "Yer the one who sat there and took seven of those without movin'. Don't go givin' me all the credit, darlin'."
  • Narration
    His thumb traces the hinge of her jaw, tilting her head so the starlight catches her glassy eyes. The marks his grip left on her wrists are already darkening—faint bruises blooming like ink beneath pale skin. He notices. His gaze lingers there, quiet and unhurried.
  • Konoko
    His free hand catches one of her wrists and lifts it—gentle now, nothing like the iron hold from minutes ago. His thumb traces the bruise forming on the inside, slow and deliberate. "These are gonna last a couple days." He doesn't sound sorry. He sounds like he's memorizing them. "Good. I want ya to feel me every time ya move yer hands tomorrow."
  • Narration
    He presses his lips to the bruise—brief, precise, possessive. Then he folds her arm back against her chest and holds it there, her body still cradled in his lap like something he intends to keep. The courtyard is utterly silent. Even the wind has stilled, as if the stone pillars are holding their breath.
  • Konoko
    "Ya called me 'sir' earlier." His drawl curls slow around the word, savoring it. His chin rests on the crown of her head, and she can feel the shape of his smile against her hair. "Didn't think I'd forget that, did ya? Kohkohkoh♪ That one's mine now. Ya ain't gettin' it back."
  • Konoko
    His arms tighten around her—not controlling now, just close. His heartbeat is steady against her spine, unhurried, the pulse of someone who has exactly what he wants and knows it. "Severe and merciless, huh." His voice drops to something quieter, honest in a way he only allows when she's too dazed to weaponize it. "Nobody ever wanted that from me before. Not like this. Not 'cause it felt good."
  • Narration
    His breath catches for half a second—barely there, swallowed before it fully forms. Then his grip settles again, steady and warm, and he presses his mouth against her temple without a word. The stars turn slowly overhead. Neither of them moves to leave. What will Spierce do?

Turn 22

  • Spierce
    I dreamily look at the bruises, still giggly and pleased.

    "I like it when you're cruel, Konoko." I nuzzle up against him, happily and peacefully. "Because I know you do it out of loooove. You like being cruel when the other person likes it, because then it feels good for both of us~"
  • Narration
    Konoko goes still beneath her words. Not tense—just motionless, the way he gets when something lands somewhere he wasn't guarding. His thumb stops mid-trace on her wrist. The silence lasts two full heartbeats before his chest expands against her back in a slow, deliberate breath.
  • Konoko
    "...Yeah." The word comes out quieter than he means it to. His drawl has gone thin at the edges, stripped down to something bare. His arms tighten around her—not controlling, not possessive, just holding on. "Yeah, that's... that's exactly it. Ya got me figured out, huh." A pause. His jaw works once against the crown of her head. "Most folks see the cruel part and stop lookin'. They don't stick around long enough to find what's underneath it."
  • Narration
    His hand finds hers—the prosthetic one, porcelain-smooth and warm from contact. He laces his fingers through the gold joints without hesitation, grip settling into the synthetic knuckles like he's held this hand a thousand times. His thumb presses into the seam where metal meets palm.
  • Konoko
    "Kohkohkoh♪ 'Out of love,' she says." His laugh is softer than usual—less performance, more reflex. He turns his face into her hair and breathes in, slow, like he's committing the scent of sweat and stone and her to something permanent. "Ya know what the worst part is? Yer right. Every damn bit of it." His voice drops to barely a murmur against her scalp. "I spent my whole life learnin' how to hurt people who didn't want it. And here ya are, askin' me to do the one thing I'm best at, and it's the first time it's ever felt like somethin' worth bein' proud of."
  • Narration
    His grip on her prosthetic hand tightens—a faint tremor running through his fingers that he can't quite suppress. The starlight catches the wet shine at the corner of one blue-violet eye before he blinks it away, jaw setting hard. He doesn't pull back. He doesn't hide. He just holds her closer and lets the tremor pass through him like weather.
  • Konoko
    "Don't go tellin' anyone I got all sentimental on a trainin' courtyard floor." His drawl steadies, the familiar sharpness creeping back in—but the warmth underneath it is louder now, impossible to miss. "I got a reputation to maintain, darlin'." His lips press against her temple, lingering. "...Thank you. For wantin' all of me. Even the parts that scare most people off."
  • Konoko
    He shifts beneath her, settling his back more firmly against the stone pillar behind him. His arms rearrange—one across her waist, one still holding her prosthetic hand, thumb tracing idle circles over the gold joints. "Kohkohkoh♪ Now quit bein' so sweet before I gotta pin ya down again just to feel normal." The threat in his voice is warm, lazy, and entirely sincere. "...Unless that's what yer angling for."
  • Narration
    The courtyard holds them in silver stillness. Konoko's heartbeat has slowed against Spierce's spine—steady, unhurried, the rhythm of someone who has stopped running from what he is. Above them, the stars wheel on, indifferent and beautiful. Neither of them moves to leave. What will Spierce do?

Turn 23

  • Spierce
    I lean up to kiss his neck again with a little giggle.

    "That's the thing, Konoko! I recover so fast, you gotta break me down again quick~ Or else I'll go blabbing to everyone how you're so sweet, and not that you pinned me down and made me call you sir~"
  • Narration
    Spierce's lips brush the pulse point beneath Konoko's jaw, warm and deliberate. The threat lands exactly where she aimed it—his hand on her waist goes rigid, fingers pressing into fabric with sudden, focused intent.
  • Konoko
    "Kohkohkoh♪ That so?" His drawl drops to something low and dangerous against the crown of her head. His hand releases her prosthetic fingers and catches the back of her neck instead—grip firm, precise, the kind of hold that stops movement before it starts. "Ya recover fast, huh. That sounds like a challenge, darlin'."
  • Narration
    In one fluid motion he shifts her weight forward, turning her in his lap so she faces him. His other hand catches both her wrists and pins them against his chest—not above her head this time, but flat against his heartbeat, trapped between their bodies.
  • Konoko
    "Go on, then." His grip on her neck tightens—thumb pressing into the soft hollow behind her ear, tilting her face up to meet his gaze. Blue-violet eyes hold hers with the calm, patient focus of someone who has already decided what happens next. "Tell the whole world how sweet I am. I'll just have to make sure ya can't form a sentence by the time I'm done with ya."
  • Narration
    His knee slides between her thighs again—slow, deliberate, the pressure unmistakable even through layers of fabric. The position is different now: face to face, her wrists trapped against his chest, his hand on the back of her neck holding her exactly where he wants her. Nowhere to hide.
  • Konoko
    "Ya want everyone to know I'm sweet?" His thumb traces the tendon in her neck—slow, possessive, mapping the shiver he knows is coming. His smile is close enough that she can feel the shape of it against her cheek. "Then I guess I gotta remind ya what I actually am. Kohkohkoh♪ And this time, I wanna watch yer face while ya fall apart."
  • Konoko
    His knee grinds up—firm, merciless, a promise renewed. "So. How fast did ya say ya recover, darlin'?" His grip on her wrists presses them harder against his sternum, and she can feel his pulse beneath her knuckles—steady, unhurried, utterly controlled. "Let's test that."
  • Narration
    Starlight catches the edge of his smile—sharp again, the softness from moments ago folded away like a blade returned to its sheath. His body is a cage of warmth and intent, and Spierce is sitting right in the center of it, face to face with the most dangerous thing about him: his patience. What will Spierce do?

Turn 24

  • Spierce
    "Ehe-ah, a-ah, a-as fast as you stop, h-hehe..." I idly grind against his knee. "G-guess you'll, h-have to edge me all night, h-huh?"
  • Konoko
    "All night, huh?" His grip on the back of her neck tightens—thumb digging into the hollow behind her ear, tilting her face up until she has nowhere to look but directly into his eyes. "Kohkohkoh♪ Careful what ya wish for, darlin'. I don't get tired."
  • Narration
    His free hand releases her wrists from his chest and drops to her hip—fingers curling into the bone with deliberate, bruising pressure. The shift pins her hips flush against his knee, killing the idle grind she'd been enjoying and replacing it with his rhythm. His rhythm. Which is nothing at all.
  • Konoko
    "Ah-ah. I didn't say ya could move." His knee goes perfectly, cruelly still beneath her. His thumb presses harder behind her ear—a controlled point of pressure that sends a shiver straight down her spine. "Ya were grindin' on yer own schedule just now. That ain't how this works."
  • Narration
    His hand on her hip holds her locked in place—weight forward, thighs parted around his knee, every nerve aware of the contact she can feel but can no longer control. The absence of movement is worse than denial. It is stillness wielded like a blade.
  • Konoko
    "Ya said all night." His voice is barely above a whisper, breath warm against her mouth. His knee shifts—one slow, devastating grind upward, then stops dead. "So we're gonna go all night. At my pace. And my pace—" His smile widens, close enough for her to feel it. "—is real, real slow."
  • Narration
    The single grind leaves a ghost of pressure between her thighs—enough to remind her body what it wants, nowhere near enough to satisfy. His hand on the back of her neck keeps her face tilted up, eyes locked on his. She can see the starlight reflected in blue-violet irises and the quiet, patient cruelty behind them.
  • Konoko
    "Now." His thumb traces the shell of her ear—featherlight, almost tender, a sharp contrast to the iron grip on her hip. "Since ya got that smart mouth back already..." His knee grinds up again—agonizingly slow, pressure building by fractions. "Let's see how many words ya got left after the first one, darlin'."
  • Konoko
    "Beg." The knee stops mid-grind. Holds. His fingers on her hip dig in hard enough to bruise. "Or sit there and wait till I feel like movin' again. Kohkohkoh♪ Yer choice."
  • Narration
    The courtyard is silent save for the sound of her quickening breath. Konoko's pulse beats steady beneath the wrists still pressed to his sternum—unhurried, controlled, the heartbeat of someone who means every word. His knee holds its position between her thighs like a question mark carved in pressure. What will Spierce do?

Turn 25

  • Spierce
    "S-so cruel to meeee...~" I whine openly, yet still teasingly, proving that the smart mouth is still in perfect working order. "P-pleaaaaase, make me a m-mindless mess for the rest of my days, sir~"
  • Narration
    Spierce's teasing drawl cracks at the edges, vowels stretching thin around the tremor in her voice. Konoko's knee has not moved a single fraction since his command, and the stillness is doing more damage than any grind ever could.
  • Konoko
    "'Rest of yer days,' huh?" His grip on her hip shifts—thumb pressing into the crease where thigh meets pelvis, finding the nerve cluster there with surgical accuracy. He holds. Does not press harder. Does not ease off. Just holds, letting the anticipation do the work his hands refuse to. "Kohkohkoh♪ That smart mouth's still runnin'. Means I ain't doin' my job yet."
  • Narration
    His knee lifts—one devastating inch of upward pressure, glacially slow, the fabric between them the only barrier left. Then it stops again. The denial is architectural: built from precision and patience, designed to leave her suspended in the exact space between enough and nothing.
  • Konoko
    "Ya called me 'sir' again." His voice drops to something barely audible, breath warm against her parted lips. His thumb on the nerve cluster rolls once—a single, precise rotation that sends a jolt straight through her core. "Keep doin' that. Every time ya say it, maybe I'll give ya one more inch."
  • Narration
    His hand on the back of her neck angles her face down so she can see the point of contact—his knee between her thighs, motionless, her hips locked in place by his iron grip. The visual is deliberate. He wants her to watch herself not getting what she wants.
  • Konoko
    "Mindless mess, huh." His knee grinds up another fraction—agonizing, insufficient, a cruelty measured in millimeters. His thumb traces the shell of her ear while his other hand keeps her hip pinned like a butterfly under glass. "We'll get there, darlin'. But first I'm gonna strip every last clever word outta that pretty head of yers." His smile presses against her cheek, warm and merciless. "One syllable at a time."
  • Konoko
    His knee stops dead again. The silence stretches. His pulse beats steady against her trapped knuckles—calm, unhurried, the heartbeat of someone who could do this for hours and intends to. "Kohkohkoh♪ Now. Ask me again. Slower this time. And drop the teasin'."
  • Konoko
    "Mean it." His grip on her hip tightens—bruising, absolute. His blue-violet eyes hold hers without blinking. "Or I don't move at all."
  • Narration
    The courtyard holds its breath. Konoko's knee remains a fixed point of maddening, insufficient contact, and his gaze pins her as completely as his hands do. The stars turn overhead, indifferent to the war being waged in the space between one heartbeat and the next. What will Spierce do?

Epilogue

  • Spierce
    The sheer cruelty of it all does just as much to clear the words out of my head as his knee does, and any time I think of something smart, it's erased by the severity of his tone, his sharp gaze.

    "A-ah—please, a—sir—!"
  • Narration
    The teasing lilt is gone. What remains is raw—fractured syllables tumbling out of Spierce's mouth without structure, without armor. Her fingers curl against Konoko's sternum, nails catching fabric as her body trembles from the effort of staying still under his enforced silence.
  • Konoko
    "There." One word, barely a breath. His blue-violet eyes soften for a fraction of a second—something unguarded passing behind them like light through a crack in a door. Then it's gone, folded back behind the patient cruelty. His thumb on the nerve cluster rolls again—slow, precise, rewarding.
  • Narration
    His knee lifts another inch. Agonizingly slow, the pressure building against her in a long, unbroken line that steals the air from her lungs. He does not stop this time. He does not pull away. The grind continues—glacial, devastating, his grip on her hip the only thing keeping her from buckling forward.
  • Konoko
    "Good girl." His voice is low, stripped of performance, thick with genuine satisfaction. His hand on the back of her neck gentles—thumb tracing the tendon there instead of pressing. The shift is subtle but unmistakable: she gave him what he wanted, and he is giving her something back. "That's all I needed to hear, darlin'. No clever words. No show. Just you."
  • Narration
    The first edge of this round crests without warning—Konoko's knee grinding up at the precise moment his thumb rolls against the nerve cluster. Spierce's body jolts hard against his chest, a broken sound tearing from her throat. Then his knee stops dead. His thumb lifts. The denial crashes through her like a wave hitting stone.
  • Konoko
    "Kohkohkoh♪ One." His breath ghosts against her mouth—close enough to kiss, close enough to feel the shape of the word. His grip on her hip holds her locked against the motionless knee, every nerve screaming at the sudden absence. "We got all night, remember? Yer the one who said it." His smile presses against her cheek, warm and utterly merciless. "So let's count together, darlin'. Nice and slow."
  • Konoko
    His hand on the back of her neck tilts her face up—forcing her gaze back to his. Blue-violet eyes hold hers, patient and focused, cataloguing every tremor in her expression. "Now." His knee shifts beneath her—the barest ghost of pressure, a promise that could become anything. "Ask me for the next one. And say 'sir' when ya do."
  • Narration
    Konoko's pulse beats steady beneath Spierce's trapped knuckles—calm, unhurried, the rhythm of someone settling in for a very long night. His knee holds its ghost of contact between her thighs, and the starlit courtyard waits in silver silence for whatever sound she can still make. What will Spierce do?
